ORDER DENYING PART AND GRANTING IN PART MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TERRY BUTTS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING INSTRUCTED THE PARTIES TO APPEAR UNLESS THE MOTION HAD BEEN RESOLVED. THE MATTER IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY THE PARTIES. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF TERRY BUTTS' MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DRAFTING HISTORY. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO FURTHER PMQ TESTIMONY BY MR SHEERER AS TO CATEGORIES 2 AND 3. DEFENDANTS MAY SELECT A DIFFERENT DEPONENT IF THEY SO CHOOSE. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO COMPEL OTHER DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT CITED SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE NOT ALREADY BEEN PRODUCED, AND PLAINTIFF HAS OTHER REMEDIES SUCH AS INTERROGATORIES AND DEPOSING A CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS TO OBTAIN MORE INFORMATION ABOUT THE DEFENDANTS' DOCUMENT ID SYSTEM. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO HAVE 30 DAYS TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)

Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions, Production Of Documents At Deposition And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. DEMIAN I. OKSENENDLER, ESQ. of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler (Tel: 415-733-1050) appearing for plaintiff Terry Butts;
Atty. RANDY A. MOSS, ESQ. of Demler, Armstrong & Rowland, LLP (Tel: 415-757-3488) appearing for defendants Truck Insurance Exchange and Mid-Century Insurance Company.

PLAINTIFF TERRY BUTTS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear unless the motion had been resolved. The matter is argued and submitted by the parties. The Court rules as follows: Plaintiff Terry Butts' motion to compel is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to drafting history. The motion is granted as to further PMQ testimony by Mr Sheerer as to categories 2 and 3. Defendants may select a different deponent if they so choose. The motion is denied as to plaintiff's request to compel other documents. Plaintiff has not cited specific documents that have not already been produced, and plaintiff has other remedies such as interrogatories and deposing a custodian of records to obtain more information about the defendants' document ID system. The motion is denied as to plaintiff's request for sanctions. The Court grants defendants' request to have 30 days to comply with the court's order. Plaintiff's counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-09-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Complaint.

Defendants Truck Insurance Exchange and Mid-Century Insurance Company's motion for leave to file their proposed first amended answer to the complaint filed by plaintiff Terry Butts is granted. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage of a case. Because trial is still several months away and all or nearly all of the new allegations were either previously known to Mr. Butts and/or are legal issues, there is no legal prejudice to Mr. Butts if the motion is granted. Therefore, any lack of diligence by defendants in seeking to amend their answer is an insufficient ground to deny leave to amend.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
